silva2008.title: The Persistence of Stereotyped Dialect Features among Portuguese-American Immigrants from São Miguel, Azores
silva2008.pagination: Running head `Journal of Portuguese Linguistics, 7-1 (2008), 3-21` with body folio in the header (`4  David J. Silva`); `page` records that printed folio. The examples used here are the emblematic front-rounded set in list (1) and the chain-shift set in the abstract/list (2), pages 3-4.
silva2008.describes: Micaelense (São Miguel, Azores) European Portuguese — the front rounded vowels [y] < stressed /u/ and [ø] < the diphthongs oi/ou, plus the tonic-vowel chain shift

mikolajczak2014.title: Caraterísticas fonéticas do Português da Ilha Terceira
mikolajczak2014.pagination: Multi-article volume; printed folio is in the running head (`418  Sylwia Mikołajczak` / `Caraterísticas fonéticas do Português da Ilha Terceira  419`) and differs from the PDF page index (front matter precedes p.417). `page` is the printed folio. Source marks stress with an apostrophe AFTER the onset ([pw'orku]), normalized to the IPA stress mark before the stressed syllable.
mikolajczak2014.describes: Terceirense (Ilha Terceira, central Azores) — crescent/anti-hiatic on-glide diphthongisation and the a-metaphony before final -o; explicitly NOT the São-Miguel [u]>[y] fronting

watson2002.title: The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic

watson2002.pagination: Printed folio in the running head; printed page = PDF page index − 15 (verified on printed pages 9, 12, 16-17, 273-274, 279-280). The PDF text layer is font-mangled, so every row was read from a render of the printed page, never from extracted text.
watson2002.describes: Cairene (Egyptian) and Ṣanʿānī (Yemeni) Arabic, in a comparative frame that also cites Moroccan (Lmnabha) forms

baxter1988.title: A Grammar of Kristang (Malacca Creole Portuguese)
baxter1988.venue: Pacific Linguistics Series B-95, Australian National University
baxter1988.pagination: Printed folio in the page header of the ANU scan; `page` records the printed folio. The scan is missing printed pp. 33-48 (one gathering); no row cites those pages. OCR frequently drops Baxter's acute stress diacritic — rows whose stress mark was restored from his penultimate default are capped at confidence medium.
baxter1988.describes: Kristang / Papia Kristang (Malacca Creole Portuguese), ch. 2 phonology

brissos2018.title: Proposta de reformulação da caracterização dialetal do noroeste português
brissos2018.venue: Estudos de Lingüística Galega, Volume especial I (2018):193-208
brissos2018.pagination: Running foot `© 2018 Estudos de lingüística galega. Volume especial I: 193-208` with the body folio in the header/footer (e.g. `Proposta de reformulação ... 199`, `200  F. Brissos`); `page` records that printed folio. The PDF page index runs 192 behind the printed page (printed 193 = PDF page 1). Examples are the diphthongization set of §2.1-2.2, printed pp.199-201, reproduced from Brissos & Rodrigues (2016).
brissos2018.describes: The diphthongizing 'variedade do noroeste' (Cintra's Baixo-Minho / Douro-Litoral, extended north) — tonic-vowel diphthongization of both close and open mids, the within-axis subtype (RD1: [i̯e i̯ɛ], [u̯o u̯ɔ]) and the toward-central subtype (RD2: [eɐ̯ ɛɐ̯], [oɐ̯ ɔɐ̯]), plus the marginal /i/ -> [ei̯]

navas2011.title: El barranqueño: un modelo de lenguas en contacto
navas2011.venue: Editorial Complutense / Centro de Linguística da Universidade de Lisboa, Madrid, 320 pp. (ISBN 978-84-9938-099-5)
navas2011.pagination: Open-access scan on Docta Complutense (handle hdl.handle.net/20.500.14352/46845). The printed folio sits in the page running foot and coincides with the PDF page index (verified: PDF page 49 = folio 49, 50 = folio 50, 52 = folio 52, 53 = folio 53). The book uses a Spanish (RFE-influenced) phonetic notation: primary stress is an apostrophe placed BEFORE the stressed syllable (normalized to IPA ˈ), and [∫]=ʃ, [γ]=ɣ, [δ]=ð, [β]=β, with final unstressed <a> written [α]/[a] and read [ɐ]; the `ipa` field carries the IPA normalization and `source_notation` keeps the book's glyphs verbatim.
navas2011.describes: Barranquenho (ext-PT-x-barrancos) — the Portuguese-Spanish contact variety of Barrancos (Baixo Alentejo, on the Extremadura/Andalusia border); Alentejo-Portuguese phonetic base with southern-peninsular Spanish overlay (coda /s/ aspiration, betacism, final-vowel apocope). Already cited by the ext-PT-x-barrancos spec.

aljamaan2026.venue: Journal of Language Teaching and Research 17(1), 98-111
aljamaan2026.pagination: `page` is the printed journal folio in the running head (98-111), which is PDF page index + 97. Prosody study (F0/boundary tones); segmental transcriptions are loose transliteration, so no segmental gold rows are harvested from it.
